items.prompt_text: Which of the following interpretations of the plot is not valid?

The typical 4 cylinder car gets better mileage than the typical 6 cylinder car, which gets better mileage than the typical 8 cylinder car.
Most of the 4 cylinder cars get better mileage than even the most efficient 8 cylinder cars.
The variability in mileage of 8 cylinder cars is smaller than the variability in mileage of 4 cylinder cars.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following interpretations of the plot is valid?

There are more SUVs than non-SUVs across all cylinder types.
There is more variability in 6-cylinder non-SUVs than in any other type of car.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following interpretations of the plot is valid?

The median length of not spam emails is greater than that of spam emails.
The IQR of email length of spam is greater than that of not spam.

items.prompt_text: Which interpretation of these faceted histograms is not correct?
choices: The most common value of exclaim_mess in both classes of email is zero (a log(exclaim_mess) of -4.6 after adding .01).
There are more cases of spam in this dataset than not spam.
Even after a transformation, the distribution of exclaim_mess in both classes of email is right-skewed.
The typical number of exclamations in the not spam group appears to be slightly higher than in the spam group.

An email without an image is more likely to be not spam than spam.

Given that an email contains a big number, it is more likely to be not spam.
Within both spam and not spam, the most common number is a small one.

items.prompt_text: Choose the correct interpretation of this value.
choices: People who graduate from high school are less likely to be poor.
Counties with lower high school graduation rates are likely to have lower poverty rates.
Counties with lower high school graduation rates are likely to have higher poverty rates.

Having a higher percentage of high school graduates in a county results in that county having lower poverty rates.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following conclusions is not a valid statistical interpretation of these results?

Staying in the womb longer causes babies to be heavier when they are born.
These data suggest that babies with longer gestational periods tend to be heavier at birth, but there are many potential confounding factors that were not taken into account.
items.correct_choice: Staying in the womb longer causes babies to be heavier when they are born.

exactly 10.0%
expected to be about 10.0%
expected to be about 11.7%

items.prompt_text: The author is arguing that...
choices: Because of regression to the mean, an outstanding basketball player is likely to have sons that are as good at basketball as him.
Because of regression to the mean, an outstanding basketball player is likely to have sons that are not good at basketball.
Because of regression to the mean, an outstanding basketball player is likely to have sons that are good at basketball, but not as good as him.
Linear regression is incapable of evaluating musical or athletic talent.
items.correct_choice: Because of regression to the mean, an outstanding basketball player is likely to have sons that are good at basketball, but not as good as him.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is the correct interpretation of the slope coefficient?
choices: Among U.S. counties, each additional percentage point increase in the poverty rate is associated with about a 0.591 percentage point decrease in the mean high school graduation rate.
Among U.S. counties, each additional percentage point increase in the high school graduation rate is associated with about a 0.591 percentage point decrease in the mean poverty rate.
Among U.S. counties, each additional percentage point increase in the high school graduation rate is associated with a 0.591 percentage point decrease in the poverty rate for each school.
Among U.S. counties, a 1% increase in the high school graduation rate is associated with about a 0.591% decrease in the poverty rate.
items.correct_choice: Among U.S. counties, each additional percentage point increase in the high school graduation rate is associated with about a 0.591 percentage point decrease in the mean poverty rate.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following mistakes in interpretation has the politician made?
choices: Implying that the regression model establishes a cause-and-effect relationship.
Switching the role of the response and explanatory variables.
Confusing percentage change with percentage point change.
All of the above.
items.correct_choice: All of the above.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

None of the above.
items.correct_choice: None of the above.

The model explains about 4.67% of the variability in poverty rate among counties.
The model correctly predicted the poverty rate of 4.67% of the counties.

items.prompt_text: How should this value be interpreted?

46.4% of the variability in poverty rate among U.S. counties can be explained by high school graduation rate.

The expected price of a used MarioKart is $5.59 less than that of a new one with the same number of wheels.

items.prompt_text: Choose the correct interpretation of this slope coefficient.
choices: For every additional thousand dollars of salary for teachers in a particular state, the expected SAT score for a student from that state is about 3 points lower.

For every additional thousand dollars of salary for teachers in a particular state, the expected SAT score for a student from that state is about 3 points higher, after controlling for the percentage of students taking the SAT.
The average SAT score in richer states is about 3 points higher.
items.correct_choice: For every additional thousand dollars of salary for teachers in a particular state, the expected SAT score for a student from that state is about 3 points higher, after controlling for the percentage of students taking the SAT.

items.prompt_text: This interpretation is false because.
choices: The coefficient on duration is negative.
Smaller coefficients are more important.
The coefficients have different units (dollars per day and dollars per dollar, respectively) and so they are not directly comparable.
The intercept coefficient is much bigger, so it is the most important one.
items.correct_choice: The coefficients have different units (dollars per day and dollars per dollar, respectively) and so they are not directly comparable.

items.prompt_text: This interpretation is correct â¦
choices: For each additional day the auction lasts, the expected final price declines by $1.51, after controlling for starting price.
For each additional dollar of starting price, the expected final price increases by $0.23, after controlling for the duration of the auction.
The duration of the auction is a more important determinant of final price than starting price, because the coefficient is larger.
The average auction lasts 51 days.
items.correct_choice: For each additional dollar of starting price, the expected final price increases by $0.23, after controlling for the duration of the auction.

items.prompt_text: Choose the right interpretation of beta the coefficient on condUsed
choices: The expected premium for new (relative to used) MarioKarts is $8.95, after controlling for the duration and starting price of the auction.
The expected premium for used (relative to new) MarioKarts is $8.95, after controlling for the duration and starting price of the auction.
For each additional day the auction lasts, the expected final price declines by $8.95, after controlling for starting price and condition.

Each additional wheel costs exactly $6.72.

Each additional wheel is associated with an increase in the expected auction price of $6.72, after controlling for auction duration, starting price, number of bids, and the condition of the item.

Restaurant and Price.
Price and Food.
Price and East.

items.prompt_text: Identify the statement that is FALSE:
choices: Each additional rating point of food quality is associated with a $2.88 increase in the expected price of meal, after controlling for location.
The premium for an Italian restaurant in NYC associated with being on the east side of 5th Avenue is $1.46, after controlling for the quality of the food.
The change in the coefficient of food from $2.94 in the simple linear model to $2.88 in this model has profound practical implications for restaurant owners.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The premium for being on the East side of 5th Avenue is just less than a dollar, after controlling for the quality of food and service.

The impact of location is relatively small, since one additional rating point of either food or service would result in a higher expected price than moving a restaurant from the West side to the East side.
The expected price of a meal on the East side is about 96% of the cost of a meal on the West side, after controlling for the quality of food and service.
items.correct_choice: The expected price of a meal on the East side is about 96% of the cost of a meal on the West side, after controlling for the quality of food and service.

items.prompt_text: Which of the following interpretations is invalid?

Service is not an important factor in determining the price of a meal.
None of the above.

topic_tags: Which of the following interpretations is invalid?

items.prompt_text: Which would be a good alternative hypothesis?

items.prompt_text: Generally, if $n$ represents the size of the original sample, how many observations should we resample with replacement when bootstrapping?
choices: Resample fewer than $n$ observations because otherwise every sample will include the same observations.

Resample more than $n$ observations in order to minimize the variability.

items.prompt_text: What is your best guess at the confidence level used in these intervals?

items.prompt_text: Which of the following statements is correct?
choices: Once the rule-of-thumb condition is violated, the approximation method instantly becomes extremely inaccurate.
At a very small sample size, both approaches will perform poorly. Inference on very small data sets is not recommended!
In traditional surveys and scientific research, the bootstrap approach is more commonly used than the approximation approach.
items.correct_choice: At a very small sample size, both approaches will perform poorly. Inference on very small data sets is not recommended!
topic_tags: Which of the following statements is correct?

items.prompt_text: Using an alpha level of .05, what is your decision regarding the null hypothesis?
choices: The p-value < alpha, thus the data is consistent with the null hypothesis so I fail to reject it as a reasonable explanation.
The p-value < alpha, thus the data is inconsistent with the null hypothesis so I reject it as a reasonable explanation.
The p-value > alpha, thus the data is consistent with the null hypothesis so I fail to reject it as a reasonable explanation.
The p-value > alpha, thus the data is inconsistent with the null hypothesis so I reject it as a reasonable explanation.
items.correct_choice: The p-value > alpha, thus the data is consistent with the null hypothesis so I fail to reject it as a reasonable explanation.
topic_tags: Using an alpha level of .05, what is your decision regarding the null hypothesis

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is FALSE regarding contingency tables?
choices: They are a common method of displaying categorical data.
They have one variable across the columns and another variable along the rows.
They have the variables across the columns and observations down the rows.
They should be transformed to a tidy format for analysis.
items.correct_choice: They have the variables across the columns and observations down the rows.
topic_tags: Which of the following is FALSE regarding contingency tables?

items.prompt_text: Select the correct answer
choices: Since both p-values are above 0.05, we fail to reject the hypotheses that both military spending and spending on space exploration are independent of political party.

The data set is consistent with the hypothesis that there is no relationship between political party and space exploration spending, but does suggest a relationship between party and spending on the military.
The data set is inconsistent with the hypothesis that there is no relationship between both military spending and party as well as spending for space exploration and party.

The data set is consistent with the hypothesis that there is no relationship between political party and military spending, but does suggest a relationship between party and spending on space exploration.
items.correct_choice: The data set is consistent with the hypothesis that there is no relationship between political party and space exploration spending, but does suggest a relationship between party and spending on the military.

items.prompt_text: The chi-squared approximation method will only be accurate for one of them. Which one?
choices: ds1
ds2
ds3
items.correct_choice: ds3

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is FALSE regarding the iran data set?
choices: Each row refers to a single ballot cast.
The data set has 9 variables on 366 observations.
The variables contain vote counts for various candidates as well as categorical data for the city and province names.
There appear to be four candidates represented in this data set.
items.correct_choice: Each row refers to a single ballot cast.

items.prompt_text: Based on your p-value of approximately 0.006, which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: If the null hypothesis is true, the probability of observing a test statistic more extreme than the observed statistic is 0.006.
This data is consistent with a model in which Benford's Law applies to vote totals from the 2009 Iranian election.
The p-value is a measure of the consistency between a data set and a particular hypothesis.
items.correct_choice: This data is consistent with a model in which Benford's Law applies to vote totals from the 2009 Iranian election.
topic_tags: Based on your p-value of approximately 0.006, which of the following statements

items.prompt_text: Determine which of the following statements is FALSE
choices: There are 1386 observations on 5 variables.
The columns contain the name of the office, the candidate, the party, the county, and the votes cast, all of which are categorical data.
Each row refers to candidate pair (President/Vice President) in a single county.
There appear to be 14 distinct categories in the candidate column.
items.correct_choice: The columns contain the name of the office, the candidate, the party, the county, and the votes cast, all of which are categorical data.

items.prompt_text: Based on the null distribution that you constructed in the last exercise and the p-value (which you can compute using null and chi_obs_stat in your workspace here), which of the following statements is FALSE?
choices: The low p-value indicates that if in fact there was election fraud in Iowa, there would be a very small probability of observing this data or more extreme.

Because the observed statistic is far into the tails of the null distribution, this indicates that your data is inconsistent with the null hypothesis that Benford's Law applies to vote totals in Iowa.
The high p-value indicates a high probability that that our null hypothesis (that Benford's Law applies to vote totals in Iowa) is true.
items.correct_choice: The high p-value indicates a high probability that that our null hypothesis (that Benford's Law applies to vote totals in Iowa) is true.

items.prompt_text: Which method is more appropriate for calculating the bootstrap confidence interval for the median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan?
choices: Percentile method.
Standard error method.
items.correct_choice: Standard error method.
topic_tags: Which method is more appropriate for calculating the bootstrap confidence interv

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is false about the distribution of visits?
choices: On average, mothers visit the doctor approximately 12 times throughout pregnancy.
The distribution of number of doctor's visits is unimodal and roughly symmetric.
The median number of doctor's visits throughout the pregnancy is roughly equal to the mean number of visits.
The number of doctor's visits for one of the mothers in the dataset is missing.
items.correct_choice: The number of doctor's visits for one of the mothers in the dataset is missing.
topic_tags: Which of the following is false about the distribution of visits?

items.prompt_text: What is the conclusion of the hypothesis test at the 5% significance level?
choices: We reject $H_0$ since the p-value is above the significance level, and conclude that the data provide convincing evidence that median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan is greater than $2500.

We fail to reject $H_0$ since the p-value is above the significance level, and conclude that the data provide convincing evidence that median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan is greater than $2500.

We reject $H_0$ since the p-value is below the significance level, and conclude that the data do not provide convincing evidence that median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan is greater than $2500.

We fail to reject $H_0$ since the p-value is above the significance level, and conclude that the data do not provide convincing evidence that median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan is greater than $2500.
items.correct_choice: We fail to reject $H_0$ since the p-value is above the significance level, and conclude that the data do not provide convincing evidence that median rent of 1 BR apartments in Manhattan is greater than $2500.
topic_tags: What is the conclusion of the hypothesis test at the 5% significance level?

items.prompt_text: Which of the following describes best why we might need to use the t-distribution for inference?
choices: When the population distribution is not known
When the population standard deviation is unknown
When the sample is not random
When the sample is not representative of the population
items.correct_choice: When the population standard deviation is unknown

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is false about confidence intervals when all else is held constant?
choices: As the confidence level increases, the margin of error of the interval increases as well.
As the confidence level increases, the width of the interval increases as well.
As the sample size increases, the margin of error of the interval increases as well.
As the sample size increases, the precision of the interval increases as well.
items.correct_choice: As the sample size increases, the margin of error of the interval increases as well.
topic_tags: Which of the following is false about confidence intervals when all else is held

items.prompt_text: Which of the following is the correct definition of the p-value of this hypothesis test?

The probability that the median math score is higher than the median science score.

The probability of getting a random sample of 200 high school students where there is no difference between the median math and science scores, if in fact the median difference between their math and science test scores is at least 1.

The probability of getting a random sample of 200 high school students where the median difference between their math and science test scores is at least 1, if in fact there is no difference between the median math and science scores.
items.correct_choice: The probability of getting a random sample of 200 high school students where the median difference between their math and science test scores is at least 1, if in fact there is no difference between the median math and science scores.

items.prompt_text: Should the researchers run a test with a one- or two-sided alternative hypothesis?
choices: one-sided because the researchers know studying leads to higher test scores.
one-sided because the researchers are trying to demonstrate a positive association.
two-sided because the researchers have no pre-conceived notion of whether or not studying is associated with test scores.
two-sided because the researchers don't want to perform data-snooping.

Bigger sample size, larger variability around the line, decreased range of explanatory variable.

items.prompt_text: What can we conclude based on the p-value associated with the twins data?
choices: If there were no association between foster and biological twin IQ (no nature) in the population, we would be extremely unlikely to have collected a sample of data like we did.
A biological twin's IQ being higher causes a foster twin's IQ to be higher.
Biological twins' IQs are higher than foster twins' IQs, on average.
Given the data, the probability of biological and foster twins' IQs being unrelated is close to zero.
items.correct_choice: If there were no association between foster and biological twin IQ (no nature) in the population, we would be extremely unlikely to have collected a sample of data like we did.

items.prompt_text: What are the appropriate conclusions of this study?
choices: Zero is not a plausible value for the population slope, one is a plausible value for the population slope.
Zero is not a plausible value for the estimated slope, one is a plausible value for the estimated slope.
Zero is a plausible value for the population slope, one is not a plausible value for the population slope.
Zero is a plausible value for the estimated slope, one not is a plausible value for the estimated slope.

items.prompt_text: Why does it matter if the sampling distribution is accurate?
choices: If the distribution is wrong, the software will not compute a p-value.
If the distribution is wrong, the p-value will not represent the probability of the data given the null hypothesis is true.
If the distribution is wrong, the CI procedure will not capture the true parameter in 95% of samples.
All of the above.
Some of the above.
items.correct_choice: Some of the above.
topic_tags: Why does it matter if the sampling distribution is accurate?

items.prompt_text: What is the appropriate interpretation of the coefficient on log(sqft)?

choices: For every one unit increase in Service, the predicted average Price is expected to increase by 0.135.
For every one unit increase in Service, the predicted average Price is expected to increase by 0.135, given fixed values of Food and Decor.
For every one unit increase in Service, the predicted average Price is expected to increase by 0.135, for any possible value of Food and Decor.
Given that Food and Decor are in the model, Service is not significant, and we cannot know whether it has effect on modeling Price
